dialogue.narrator_ch0_start: The hall at Tara is quiet now, and I am the reason for the quiet. Murchadh mac Fáelán did not need to raise his voice — the board said everything he wished to say, and the board said it plainly.
dialogue.narrator_ch0_post_scripted: I walk out of Tara with the clothes on my back and the game in my head. There are roads west — there are always roads west — and people who have not yet heard my name said wrong.
dialogue.narrator_ch1_start: Connacht. The Atlantic comes in hard off the headlands and everything here smells of salt and old rain. These people have their own games, their own grudges. I am nobody here, and that is where I begin.
dialogue.narrator_ch1_end: Fiachra watched me go without a word, and I felt his eyes on my back for a long while. The road east is longer than the road I came by. Murchadh is at the end of it, and he does not know I am walking.
dialogue.murchadh_pre_scripted: I have heard your name. I expected something more.
dialogue.murchadh_post_loss_scripted: Go west. There may be people there who will play you.
dialogue.seanan_pre_first: Sit, sit — I was going to mend the net but it can wait. You look like someone who has been walking a while.
dialogue.seanan_post_win_first: Ha. Well. The fish will not mock me for it, I suppose. You play like someone who has lost before and did not forget it.
dialogue.seanan_post_loss_first: There it is. I do not always see the whole board but sometimes the board shows me anyway. Stay and eat, if you like.
dialogue.seanan_pre_retry: Back already. I thought you might be. Here — the board is where you left it.
dialogue.seanan_post_win_retry: You came back quieter than you left, and you played quieter too. That is how the sea teaches — not with words.
dialogue.seanan_feud_pre: I did not expect to see you here again, not after the way that went. Sit then — but I am not going easy.
dialogue.seanan_feud_post_win: There — now that is a thing. I will not hold what was before against what just happened. The board has its own memory.
dialogue.seanan_feud_post_loss: Again. I do not know what you are trying to prove but the board keeps saying you have not proved it.
dialogue.seanan_nemesis_pre: I have said nothing to anyone about this and I will not. But you keep coming back, and I keep winning, and that is between us.
dialogue.seanan_nemesis_post_win: Well. There it is. The sea does not stay angry — that was never the sea's way. Go on, then.
dialogue.brigid_pre_first: I do not play in haste and I do not play in company I do not trust. Sit with me a while first. Then we will see.
dialogue.brigid_post_win_first: You protect the King as though you have been the King yourself once. That is not a common thing.
dialogue.brigid_post_loss_first: You opened too early. Patience is not the absence of movement — it is knowing which movement to withhold.
dialogue.brigid_pre_retry: You have thought about it since. I can see that. Good — thinking is most of the game.
dialogue.brigid_post_win_retry: You held the thing you learned and did not let the losing spoil it. That is the harder discipline, and you had it.
dialogue.brigid_feud_pre: I gave you time and I gave you the game straight. What you took from it was not what I offered. Come — we will see if you have corrected that.
dialogue.brigid_feud_post_win: You found the discipline. That is what I was waiting for — not the win, the discipline behind it. We are done with this.
dialogue.brigid_feud_post_loss: It is the same gap, in a different place. You have not found the root of it yet. I have nothing to add.
dialogue.brigid_nemesis_pre: I do not hold things with heat. I hold them with stillness. You have had a long time to feel that stillness and it has not moved you. Sit.
dialogue.brigid_nemesis_post_win: I misjudged how long it would take you. That is my error, not yours. You have the thing now. Keep it.
dialogue.tadhg_pre_first: I have beaten three men today already and the night is young. Set the board — I do not have all evening.
dialogue.tadhg_post_win_first: Right, right — fair enough. Who sent you? Séanán? He has the look of someone who teaches quietly.
dialogue.tadhg_post_loss_first: You held too long — a man who will not press his advantage is a man asking to lose. Come back when you have the stomach for it.
dialogue.tadhg_pre_retry: Again. Fine. This time I will not make it easy.
dialogue.tadhg_post_win_retry: All right, all right — you came back and you came back better, I will give you that much and not a word more.
dialogue.tadhg_feud_pre: I told everyone in this house you would come back and lose again. Do not make me wrong.
dialogue.tadhg_feud_post_win: Right. Once. Do not think that means anything. Get out of my hall before I change my mind about being gracious.
dialogue.tadhg_feud_post_loss: There it is. Twice. Walk away and do not come back until you have something new.
dialogue.tadhg_nemesis_pre: I have beaten you enough times that your name in my hall is a joke. Prove it wrong or do not sit down.
dialogue.tadhg_nemesis_post_win: You won. In my hall. I will not pretend that did not happen. I will not give you more than that.
dialogue.fiachra_pre_first: I have heard three different things about you from three different people, and none of them agreed. Sit — I want to see which one is true.
dialogue.fiachra_post_win_first: There is someone east of here who has not seen that. I wonder if you will get there before he hears of it.
dialogue.fiachra_post_loss_first: You protect the centre well enough. But you read the board — you do not read the player. That is the gap.
dialogue.fiachra_pre_retry: You came back. That is the first thing I needed to know about you.
dialogue.fiachra_post_win_retry: The second thing I needed to know about you — you came back and you won. The road east is yours.
dialogue.fiachra_feud_pre: You came back with something on your face that was not there before. I do not think that is the board you are angry at. Sit — let us find out.
dialogue.fiachra_feud_post_win: That is what I was watching for — not the move, what came before the move. You found something. The rest of this is finished.
dialogue.fiachra_feud_post_loss: You are carrying something into the game that does not belong there. Until you set it down you will keep losing it for reasons that have nothing to do with the board.
dialogue.fiachra_nemesis_pre: I have told no one what I think is happening with you. There is still time to resolve it on the board. This is the last time I will offer that.
dialogue.fiachra_nemesis_post_win: I wondered if you would get here. I stopped wondering a while ago and started waiting. The road east is clearer now — you have settled the thing that was clouding it.
dialogue.tutorial_step_1: The board is set. You are the Defender — your pieces are the light stones. The tall one in the centre is the King.
dialogue.tutorial_step_2: Pieces move in straight lines — up, down, left, right. As far as the path is clear. Tap the piece I have marked, then tap where you would like it to go.
dialogue.tutorial_step_3: The centre square — the Throne — and the four corners are special. Only the King may enter them.
dialogue.tutorial_step_4: When you trap an enemy piece between two of yours, it is taken. Move here — watch.
dialogue.tutorial_step_5: But your pieces can be trapped the same way. Be careful where you leave them.
dialogue.tutorial_step_6: The King must escape to any corner of the board. That is how you win. But if the enemy surrounds him on all four sides, you lose. They are closing in — move your King.
dialogue.tutorial_step_7: The path is open. Move the King to the corner.
dialogue.tutorial_step_8: The King has escaped. You understand the shape of it now. The rest you will learn by playing.
